original_citation: Mazzotta C, et al. 2024. American Journal of Ophthalmology. 259. 12-21.
title_en: Precision Medicine in Keratoconus: Customized vs Conventional Cross-linking Outcomes
title_zh: 圓錐角膜精準醫療：客製化與傳統型交聯術成效對照研究
tldr_en: This randomized trial compared traditional cross-linking with a customized version that targets specific weak areas of the cornea. The customized group showed significantly better corneal flattening and improved visual quality after one year.
tldr_zh: 這項隨機對照試驗比較了傳統型與客製化角膜交聯術，後者針對角膜特定脆弱區域進行治療。結果顯示，客製化組在一年後的角膜平坦化程度與視覺品質均有顯著提升。

research_design.sample: 60 eyes of 60 patients with progressive keratoconus, randomized into two treatment groups.
research_design.method: A randomized clinical trial comparing Topography-Guided (Customized) CXL with the standard Dresden protocol over a 12-month period.
research_design.key_finding: The customized group achieved a mean Kmax reduction of 2.1 Diopters compared to only 0.8 Diopters in the conventional group (p < 0.05).
research_design.limitation: The study was limited by a relatively small sample size and a follow-up period restricted to one year.

article_en:
The management of progressive keratoconus has long relied on the standard Dresden protocol for corneal collagen cross-linking (CXL). While effective at stabilizing the cornea, this 'one-size-fits-all' approach applies uniform ultraviolet-A energy across the entire corneal surface. However, keratoconus is inherently asymmetrical, with localized thinning and steepening. This clinical trial investigated whether a customized, topography-guided approach—which concentrates energy on the most ectatic regions—could provide superior morphological and functional outcomes compared to the conventional method.

The researchers enrolled 60 patients and utilized a randomized design to ensure that baseline characteristics were balanced. In the customized group, surgeons used advanced corneal mapping to create a specific irradiance pattern, delivering higher energy to the 'cone' while sparing the thinner peripheral regions. The control group received the standard treatment of 3 mW/cm² for 30 minutes. The primary goal was to compare the flattening effect, measured as the change in maximum keratometry (Kmax), and improvements in uncorrected visual acuity over twelve months.

The results revealed a clear advantage for the customized protocol. At the one-year mark, the topography-guided group showed a significant regression in corneal steepness, with Kmax values decreasing by an average of 2.1 Diopters. In contrast, the conventional group only experienced a 0.8 Diopter reduction. Furthermore, patients in the customized cohort reported fewer higher-order aberrations, such as glare and ghosting, which suggests that focusing the treatment on the most distorted areas results in a smoother, more regular corneal surface.

From a methodological perspective, the study highlights the trade-off between procedural simplicity and clinical precision. The customized approach requires sophisticated planning and specialized laser equipment, which may not be accessible in all clinical settings. However, the researchers argue that the reduction in postoperative visual symptoms justifies the additional complexity. They also noted that by avoiding high-intensity exposure in thinner peripheral areas, the customized protocol might offer a higher safety profile for patients with advanced disease.

In conclusion, this trial suggests that customized CXL represents a significant step toward precision medicine in ophthalmology. While the standard protocol remains a robust option for general stabilization, topography-guided intervention offers a superior solution for patients seeking better visual quality. Future studies should explore whether these benefits persist beyond one year and whether this approach can further delay the need for corneal transplantation in severe cases.
article_zh:
漸進性圓錐角膜的治療長期以來仰賴標準的德勒斯登協議（Dresden protocol）進行角膜膠原交聯術（CXL）。雖然該方法在穩定角膜方面十分有效，但這種「一體適用」的方式是在整個角膜表面施加均勻的紫外光能量。然而，圓錐角膜本質上是不對稱的，具有局部的變薄與凸起。本臨床試驗探討了客製化的地形圖引導方案——將能量集中在最擴張的區域——是否能比傳統方法提供更優異的形態與功能成效。

研究人員招募了 60 名患者，並採用隨機設計以確保基準特徵平衡。在客製化組中，醫師利用先進的角膜製圖技術建立特定的輻照模式，將較高能量傳遞至「圓錐」部分，同時避開較薄的周邊區域。對照組則接受標準的每平方公分 3 毫瓦、持續 30 分鐘的治療。主要目標是比較十二個月內的最大角膜弧度值（Kmax）平坦化效果以及未經矯正視力的改善程度。

結果顯示客製化方案具有明顯優勢。在一年追蹤點，地形圖引導組顯示出顯著的角膜陡度回歸，Kmax 平均降低了 2.1 屈光度。相比之下，傳統組僅降低了 0.8 屈光度。此外，客製化組患者報告的高階像差（如眩光和重影）較少，這顯示將治療集中在最扭曲的區域能產生更平滑、更規則的角膜表面。

從方法論的角度來看，本研究突顯了程序簡單性與臨床精準度之間的權衡。客製化方法需要精密的規劃和專門的雷射設備，這在所有臨床環境中可能並不普及。然而，研究人員認為術後視覺症狀的減少證明了增加複雜度的價值。他們還指出，透過避免在較薄的周邊區域進行高強度照射，客製化方案可能為晚期疾病患者提供更高的安全性。

總之，這項試驗顯示客製化交聯術代表了眼科精準醫療的重要一步。雖然標準協議仍是穩定病情的穩健選擇，但地形圖引導的介入為尋求更佳視覺品質的患者提供了更優越的解決方案。未來的研究應探討這些益處是否能持續超過一年，以及這種方法是否能進一步延緩嚴重病例對角膜移植的需求。

glossary.definition_en: Specifically designed to fit the needs or requirements of a particular person.
glossary.definition_zh: 專門為特定個人的需求或要求而設計的。

glossary.definition_en: The amount of light energy that hits a surface per unit of area.
glossary.definition_zh: 每單位面積照射到表面的光能量。

glossary.definition_en: A defect in an image caused by a lens or mirror that prevents light from focusing correctly.
glossary.definition_zh: 由鏡片或鏡子引起的影像缺陷，使光線無法正確聚焦。

glossary.definition_en: A unit used to measure the power of an optical lens.
glossary.definition_zh: 用來測量光學鏡片度數的單位。

glossary.definition_en: A return to a previous or less advanced state; in medicine, a decrease in severity.
glossary.definition_zh: 回到之前的或較不先進的狀態；在醫學中指嚴重程度的降低。

glossary.definition_en: Avoiding doing something that would harm or damage something.
glossary.definition_zh: 避免做會傷害或損壞某事的事情。

quiz.q: What was the main methodological difference between the customized and conventional groups?
options: A. The customized group did not use ultraviolet light.
B. The customized group used a uniform energy pattern across the cornea.
C. The customized group focused energy on the steepest parts of the cornea.
D. The conventional group used a laser for energy delivery.
quiz.hint: Look at the second paragraph describing the surgeon's approach.
quiz.explain_zh: 正確答案是 C。文中提到客製化組使用角膜製圖技術，將較高的能量傳遞到角膜的「圓錐」（最凸起處），而非均勻分配。

quiz.q: How much more did the Kmax decrease in the customized group compared to the conventional group?
options: A. 0.8 Diopters
B. 1.3 Diopters
C. 2.1 Diopters
D. 2.9 Diopters
quiz.hint: Subtract the conventional group's reduction from the customized group's reduction.
quiz.explain_zh: 正確答案是 B。客製化組降低了 2.1D，傳統組降低了 0.8D，兩者相差 1.3D。

quiz.q: What clinical benefit did patients in the customized group report regarding their vision quality?
options: A. Better night vision in total darkness.
B. A total cure of their keratoconus.
C. Fewer visual aberrations like glare and ghosting.
D. Improved peripheral vision but worse central vision.
quiz.hint: Check the results section in the third paragraph.
quiz.explain_zh: 正確答案是 C。研究發現客製化組的高階像差（如眩光和重影）較少，視覺品質更佳。

quiz.q: An eye clinic has limited budget and can only afford one type of CXL device. Which protocol should they choose if their priority is simple procedures for general stabilization?
options: A. Topography-guided CXL.
B. Precision customized CXL.
C. The standard Dresden protocol.
D. Any protocol that uses vitamin B2.
quiz.hint: Think about the 'trade-off' mentioned in the methodological discussion.
quiz.explain_zh: 正確答案是 C。文中提到客製化方法較為複雜且需要專業設備，而標準協議（Dresden protocol）仍是穩定病情的「穩健選項」。

quiz.q: Why might a patient with very thin peripheral corneas be a better candidate for customized CXL?
options: A. Because customized CXL uses less vitamin drops.
B. Because the customized protocol can spare thinner areas from high-intensity energy.
C. Because the surgery takes much less time.
D. Because they don't need to wear glasses after surgery.
quiz.hint: Look for the safety profile discussion in the fourth paragraph.
quiz.explain_zh: 正確答案是 B。客製化方案可以針對性地避開較薄的區域施加高能量，對這類特殊患者更安全。

quiz.q: What is a significant limitation of this study that might make it hard to confirm long-term success?
options: A. It did not include any patients with progressive disease.
B. The follow-up period was only 12 months.
C. The researchers did not use a randomized design.
D. The energy dose used was too low to have any biological effect.
quiz.hint: Check the last sentence of the research design summary or the conclusion.
quiz.explain_zh: 正確答案是 B。研究的追蹤時間僅有一年，這在慢性漸進性疾病中不足以判斷治療是否具有長期的、跨越數年的穩定性。

speaking_prompt: You are a lead ophthalmologist presenting at a hospital board meeting. ① Explain the difference between standard and customized cross-linking. ② Describe the key findings regarding corneal flattening and visual quality in your recent trial. ③ Discuss the practical trade-off between equipment cost and patient outcomes. Maintain a professional and data-driven tone. Start your presentation now.
